top_signals.title: Shipowners still face ‘very risky’ Hormuz, await US-Iran truce details
top_signals.summary: Shipping companies still face high risks in transiting the Strait of Hormuz despite the announcement of an upcoming US-Iran peace deal, security officials said June 15, while warning the resumption of normal traffic could take weeks. Following over two months of negotiations, Iran and the US have agreed to sign a preliminary deal June 19 ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Egypt Clears $6 Billion in Energy Debt and Opens Door to a New Gas Boom
top_signals.summary: The announcement from Egypt’s petroleum and mineral resources minister Karim Badawi that the country has paid all its outstanding debts to foreign oil firms is as welcome to international oil companies and their governments as it is to the country itself. Egypt has become one of the West’s prime targets in the hunt for replacement gas supplies after the loss of Russian flows following the 24 February 2022 invasion of Ukraine. Officially, it holds around 93 trillion cubic feet (Tcf) of proven natural gas reserves, but unofficially, it…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Still See More Upside to Tech Trade: JPMorgan Asset Management
top_signals.summary: Aisa Ogoshi of JPMorgan Asset Management says "risk obviously is in the tech trade" due to the market concentration. But she adds that "there's more upside to the tech trade" in terms of the AI data center supply chain. Ogoshi also discusses the outlook for the Bank of Japan. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: EU adds 34 people and 47 entities to Russia sanctions list
top_signals.summary: The European Union added 34 individuals and 47 entities to its Russia sanctions list on Monday, targeting figures linked to Moscow’s shadow fleet operations and those connected to the death of opposition leader Alexei Navalny. The European Council said the expanded list covers individuals and organizations tied to Russia’s military and industrial complex, its shadow ...

top_signals.title: Wargaming for Improved Acquisition: What Does It Take?
top_signals.summary:
A few months ago, I attended a panel discussion for a wargame simulating rapid industrial mobilization for armed conflict. Conducted by a leading university, with teams composed of former senior defense officials, the game probed how government and industry collaboration would play out given minimal coordination before the onset of a crisis. On the panel, the defense leaders confessed how infrequently they engaged with industry in real life to plan for a national emergency. This declared lack of public-private planning for large-scale conflict matches what I’ve experienced as a defense planner and wargame developer: Outside of rhetorical claims at annual

top_signals.title: Greece’s defining role in the future of responsible ship recycling
top_signals.summary: For decades, Greek shipping has demonstrated an extraordinary ability to adapt, evolve and lead through every market cycle. From navigating volatile freight markets to driving fleet renewal and influencing global investment trends, Greek shipowners have consistently remained at the forefront of international shipping. Today, as environmental regulation, ESG expectations and decarbonisation pressures reshape the maritime ...

top_signals.summary: Overall credit conditions are softening in APAC with more ‘deteriorating’ 2026 sector outlooks at the mid-year update, led by sovereigns and corporates, says Fitch Ratings. top_signals.title: AI Power Hunger Sparks Push for Energy Efficiency
top_signals.summary: The surge of AI and the data center boom have started to pose challenges to the global energy system amid soaring power demand, spiking energy bills, and a higher environmental footprint. As much as AI is changing the world and the economy, it could also offer assistance to one of the energy sector’s most pressing needs in times of rising demand, uncertainty in fossil fuel supply, and inflationary and supply-chain pressures in the renewables industry—energy efficiency. AI could be the tool to help unlock additional energy gains and…

alerts.title: Baltic Dry Index Down for 2nd Day
alerts.summary: The Baltic Exchange’s dry bulk freight index, which monitors rates for ships carrying dry bulk commodities, eased for a second session on Tuesday, falling about 1.8% to 2,670 points, weighed down by the larger vessel segments. The capesize index, which typically transports 150,000-ton cargoes including iron ore and coal, dropped about 3.5% to 3,911 points; ...

alerts.title: Hokkaido tour boat operator sentenced to five years’ jail over fatal 2022 sinking
alerts.summary: Eighteen passengers and two crew members died after a tour boat sank on April 23, 2022, off the Shiretoko Peninsula in Hokkaido, with six passengers still unaccounted for.

alerts.title: Wind-assisted propulsion: Managing safety while regulation takes shape
alerts.summary: As wind-assisted propulsion scales across the global fleet, operations are advancing faster than regulation. With IMO guidance still under development, safety today rests on strong operational practice, class approval, and the ISM Code. Wind-assisted propulsion systems (WAPS) have moved beyond pilot projects. Today, ships equipped with rotors, wings, and sails are trading globally, helping owners ...
